summary: Postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script to sh from third-party domain
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js runs `execSync("npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh")`. The fallback unconditionally fetches a shell script from opencode.ai — a separate vendor from the package's own publisher (github.com/DeadJustLive/OpenPrompt-Lang) — and pipes it to `sh` with no version pin and no hash/signature verification. Whatever opencode.ai serves at that URL at the moment of install runs with the installer's privileges; the content is mutable and outside this package author's control. The primary branch additionally performs an unconsented global install of `@opencode/cli`, mutating the installer's global npm environment. Installers running `npm install openprompt-lang` have not consented to executing arbitrary third-party shell scripts or to global package installs from a different vendor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
